name: Optional Chaining & Nullish Coalescing
description: Use ?. to safely traverse nullable chains and ?? to provide defaults without swallowing falsy values like 0 or empty string.

name: Destructuring Patterns
description: Use object and array destructuring with defaults, renaming, and rest to write concise, self-documenting code.

name: Async/Await and Promise Patterns
description: Prefer async/await over raw .then() chains. Use Promise.allSettled for independent operations, Promise.any for first-success, and AbortController for cancellation.

name: ES Modules (ESM) Best Practices
description: Use named exports as the default; use default exports only for framework conventions. Dynamic import() for lazy loading. Keep circular imports impossible by design.

name: Structured Error Handling
description: Create typed error classes, always rethrow after logging, and never silence errors with empty catch blocks.

name: Immutable Data Patterns
description: Never mutate arrays or objects in place. Use spread, structuredClone, Array methods that return new arrays, and Object.freeze for constants.

name: Object.groupBy and Map.groupBy (ES2024)
description: Use Object.groupBy to bucket arrays by a key into a plain object. Use Map.groupBy when keys are non-string.

name: Closures and Module Pattern
description: Understand closures as the foundation of private state in JavaScript. Use the module pattern for encapsulation when class syntax is overkill.

name: Modern Array Methods
description: Master flat, flatMap, findLast, findLastIndex, at(), Array.from, and the ES2023 immutable mutator methods (toSorted, toReversed, with, toSpliced).

name: structuredClone for Deep Copy
description: Use structuredClone() — the native deep clone API — instead of JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()) or lodash.cloneDeep. Handles Dates, Sets, Maps, ArrayBuffers, undefined, circular (not functions).
